message:
feat(fp-columnar): add nsmallest_keep/nlargest_keep with explicit tie policy (frankenpandas-2j2j)

The existing nsmallest/nlargest shortcuts implement pandas'
keep='first' default. This adds explicit-keep variants matching the
full pandas API:

- keep='first' → stable order with ties broken by original position.
- keep='last'  → prefer later-appearing rows on ties.
- keep='all'   → extend the returned set past `n` to include every
  row tied with the boundary value.

Implemented via a shared nkeep_impl helper that annotates values
with their original index, sorts with the requested primary
direction, and uses the index as the tie-break key.

Unknown keep policies return ColumnError::Type; n=0 yields an empty
same-dtype column.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
